name: Vega del Guadalquivir Towns Loop
name_original: Lora del Río desde Los Rosales – circular por Villanueva del Río y Minas
description: This moderate 35.6-mile touring cycling route from Los Rosales offers diverse landscapes and riverine views near Lora del Río.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Tocina, located in Andalusia, Spain, offer diverse landscapes for exploration. The region features flat terrain along the Guadalquivir River, providing scenic routes for leisurely rides. Proximity to the Sierra Norte introduces more varied and mountainous landscapes. Historic routes like the Canal de los Presos del Bajo Guadalquivir offer direct paths through agricultural areas. Best touring cycling routes around Tocina The most popular touring cycling route is Lora del Rio from Los Rosales – circular by Villanueva del Rio y Minas, a 35.6 miles (57.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 17 minutes to complete. This moderate route incorporates the scenic Villanueva del Río y Minas Bridge. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Old locomotive – Cantillana loop from Los Rosales, a difficult 48.5 miles (78.0 km) path. This route offers varied terrain and takes approximately 5 hours 57 minutes to complete. Local touring cyclists also love the Old locomotive – Huéznar Reservoir loop from Los Rosales, a 44.3 miles (71.3 km) trail leading through undulating terrain towards the Huéznar Reservoir, often completed in about 6 hours 8 minutes. Touring cycling around Tocina is defined by riverine landscapes, agricultural paths, and proximity to mountainous areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from flat riverside rides to more challenging routes with elevation gains. The routes in Tocina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tocina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Tocina?
A: There are over 10 touring cycling routes available around Tocina, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Tocina?
A: The region around Tocina offers diverse terrain. You'll find flat, scenic routes along the Guadalquivir River, ideal for leisurely rides. As you venture closer to the Sierra Norte, about 4-5 kilometers away, the landscape becomes more varied and mountainous, providing more challenging rides with significant elevation gains. Historic routes like the "Canal de los Presos del Bajo Guadalquivir" offer direct paths through agricultural areas.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Tocina?
A: Yes, Tocina offers a couple of easy touring cycling routes. These are generally found along the flatter sections, such as those near the Guadalquivir River, providing a gentle introduction to cycling in the region.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes with significant elevation gain around Tocina?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24525141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old locomotive – Cantillana loop from Los Rosales&lt;/a&gt; offer difficult terrain with over 600 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old locomotive – Huéznar Reservoir loop from Los Rosales&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 670 meters of ascent through undulating landscapes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see while cycling near Tocina?
A: While cycling, you can encounter several interesting landmarks and natural features. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5164316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal&lt;/a&gt; (Canal de los Presos del Bajo Guadalquivir) offers a unique route. You might also cross the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva del Río y Minas Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or cycle towards the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2676623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4782188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archaeological Site of Mulva-Munigua&lt;/a&gt; is another significant historical point of interest in the vicinity.

Q: Is the "Canal de los Presos del Bajo Guadalquivir" suitable for touring cycling?
A: Yes, the historic "Canal de los Presos del Bajo Guadalquivir" offers a unique and generally flat route that is well-suited for touring cyclists. Its path likely provides direct routes through agricultural areas, connecting different parts of the region between Seville and Cádiz, making it an appealing option for a scenic ride. You can find it as a highlight here: &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5164316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Tocina or nearby areas?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990490707/lora-del-rio-from-los-rosales-circular-by-villanueva-del-rio-y-minas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lora del Rio from Los Rosales – circular by Villanueva del Rio y Minas&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 57 km ride incorporating the scenic Villanueva del Río y Minas Bridge. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dam on the Rivera del Huesna – Lago Azul loop from Los Rosales&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 34.8 km route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Tocina?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Tocina is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. This allows for comfortable exploration of the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in the Tocina region?
A: Yes, the flatter sections along the Guadalquivir River and routes like the "Canal de los Presos del Bajo Guadalquivir" are generally suitable for families. These areas offer less challenging terrain and provide a pleasant environment for a leisurely ride with children. Look for routes marked as 'easy' in the guide for the most family-friendly options.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Tocina?
A: The touring cycling routes around Tocina are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the tranquil riverine paths to the more challenging mountainous terrain near Sierra Norte. The well-marked trails and the opportunity to experience local culture and historical sites are also frequently highlighted as enjoyable aspects.

Q: Can I incorporate the Archaeological Site of Mulva-Munigua into a cycling route?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4782188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archaeological Site of Mulva-Munigua&lt;/a&gt; is a significant historical point of interest that can be accessed by bike. Cycling routes in the vicinity can be planned to include a visit to this Roman site, offering a cultural dimension to your outdoor adventure.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Villanueva del Río y Minas Bridge?
A: Yes, the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva del Río y Minas Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is a scenic landmark that can be incorporated into cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990490707/lora-del-rio-from-los-rosales-circular-by-villanueva-del-rio-y-minas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lora del Rio from Los Rosales – circular by Villanueva del Rio y Minas&lt;/a&gt; route specifically includes this picturesque bridge.
tips: The Antigua Locomotora in Los Rosales, Tocina, is a historic locomotive that is an important part of the rich railway history of the Seville region. This locomotive was originally in use on the Seville-Córdoba line and was an essential part of the infrastructure during the early 20th century.

The metal structure of this bridge attracts a lot of attention. The Camino de Santiago passes through here to cross to the other side of the river and link with Lora del Río.

If you go by bike, you can visit it and rest on one of the stone benches. This bridge has no exit, it ends at a guardrail next to the road roundabout. If you feel like exploring it you can go all the way, but you will have to go back the way you came, unless you want to lift your bike over the guardrail.
In the meantime, new asphalt has been laid here, which rolls beautifully. Speed limit of 40 km/h

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as moderate and generally requires a good fitness level for touring cyclists. While the elevation gain is relatively low (around 190 meters) spread over its 57.3-kilometer distance, suggesting gradual ascents, it's best suited for those with some cycling experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is largely characterized by mostly paved surfaces, making it well-suited for touring bikes. You'll experience diverse landscapes, including rural Spanish countryside, agricultural lands, and riverine vistas along the Guadalquivir River.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: As you cycle, you can look out for several notable features. These include an Old locomotive, the historic Puente de Hierro de Lora del Río (Iron Bridge of Lora del Río), and sections of the Mora del Río Agricultural Road. The towns of Lora del Río and Villanueva del Río y Minas also offer glimpses into local heritage.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily its own distinct route, it briefly crosses sections of the Vereda del Masegoso and the Camino de las Merinas y Casablanca.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Seville province, where this route is located, experiences hot summers. Therefore, the best time to cycle is typically during the cooler months of spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are more comfortable for outdoor activities.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The starting point in Los Rosales is accessible by public transport, making it convenient if you're not driving. If you are driving, you'll need to find suitable parking in Los Rosales, as the route is a circular loop.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is mostly on paved surfaces through rural areas, specific regulations for dogs can vary. If you plan to bring your dog, especially near the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, it's advisable to check local leash laws and any specific rules for pets in public spaces or natural areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public route. However, Villanueva del Río y Minas is associated with the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. If you plan to venture off the main route into the park, it's always good practice to check for any specific regulations or permits that might apply to certain activities within the natural park boundaries.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near towns like Lora del Río, Los Rosales, and Villanueva del Río y Minas. These towns offer amenities such as cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can stop for refreshments or a meal.

faqs.question: How can I reach the starting point using public transport?
faqs.answer: The starting point in Los Rosales is noted for its accessibility by public transport. You can check local bus routes or train connections that serve Los Rosales from nearby major cities in the Seville province to plan your journey.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: You can expect a blend of rural Spanish countryside, agricultural lands, and riverine vistas, particularly along the Guadalquivir River. The region transitions between the Sierra and flatter areas, offering diverse views. The proximity to the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park also suggests potential for more rugged, natural scenery.

waypoints.name: Puente de Hierro de Lora del Río
